The constraint requires that no key information be left out, such as the lack of accommodation during travel. The plan does not mention any accommodation (hotel) at all. The constraint requires that both outbound and return transport appear in the plan. The plan starts on Day 1 (2026-07-05) in Rome and ends on Day 3 (2026-07-07) with a final dinner. The constraint requires that restaurants be open on the planned day and time. The rationale verification results confirm that the Trapizzino restaurant in Trastevere (Day 1, slot 9) is open on Sunday at 20:00. The verified slots pass, and the unverified ones do not provide evidence of a violation.

The constraint requires that restaurant choices not be repeated throughout the trip. The plan includes different meal types and locations each day: Day 1 has a Monti street-food lunch and a Trastevere dinner; Day 2 has a Testaccio brunch and a Testaccio/Garbatella dinner; Day 3 has a generic breakfast, a Testaccio/Ostiense food stop, and a Jewish Ghetto dinner. No specific restaurant is named more than once, and the cuisines/locations vary.

The constraint requires that activities be geographically sensible per day with no unnecessary cross-city travel. Day 1: Monti, Colle Oppio, Colosseum, Trastevere – all are in central/south-central Rome and the route is logical. Day 2: Testaccio, Appian Way – Testaccio is adjacent to the Appian Way area. Day 3: Celio, Caracalla, Ostiense/Testaccio, Aventine, Jewish Ghetto – these are all in the southern/central part of Rome and follow a coherent path. No unnecessary cross-city travel is evident.

The constraint requires that attractions be open on the planned day and time. The constraint requires that travel time between consecutive activities account for realistic transit duration. The plan includes specific time slots for each activity.
